reasoning: C.I. Pigment Red 104 is an inorganic mixed-metal salt pigment composed of lead chromate, lead molybdate, and lead sulfate — a complex inorganic compound containing multiple metal cations (Pb²⁺) with different oxoanions (chromate, molybdate, sulfate). It fits best under mixed metal/non-metal compounds, specifically the miscellaneous category as no more specific label covers this multi-anion lead salt mixture.
substance_name: C.I. Pigment Red 104 (or: lead chromate/molybdate/sulfate; C.I. No. 77605)
